executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware wave with 100+ organizations publicly listed as victims in the last 48 hours, spanning healthcare, education, municipal government, and critical infrastructure. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security has issued an urgent advisory for three critical vulnerabilities in Cisco ASA and FTD devices (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363) that require immediate patching. High-profile Canadian victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate ransomware groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, National Money Mart (database leaked), and Peterborough Public Health. The Qilin ransomware group alone is responsible for 15+ Canadian victims, while Clop has compromised 14 organizations and Play has hit 9 targets.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is facing a critical ransomware crisis with over 100 organizations publicly compromised in 48 hours, representing the highest concentration of Canadian victims in a single reporting period. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL25-012 warning of critical vulnerabilities in Cisco ASA and FTD devices requiring immediate patching. Victims span healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, BioPharma Services, Labtician Ophthalmics), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Collge Superieur De Montreal, Teccart), municipal government (Municipality of North Perth, Peterborough Public Health), and critical infrastructure (Eeyou Communications Network).
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose was hit by two separate ransomware groups (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters). Active Green + Ross and Kids & Company (consumer services) were also compromised.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered a database leak by the Everest ransomware group. Morison Insurance Brokers was compromised by Sinobi ransomware. Multiple accounting firms were hit including Pewarchuk CPA and MG Chartered Professional Accountant.

report_2w.executive_summary: Multiple critical vulnerabilities are under active exploitation this week. CVE-2026-35616 (Fortinet FortiClientEMS, CVSS 9.1) and CVE-2026-1731 (Bomgar RMM) are both confirmed exploited and added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. CVE-2026-34197 (Apache ActiveMQ) is being actively exploited after lying dormant for 13 years. Separately, Vercel disclosed a significant breach by ShinyHunters via compromised AI tool OAuth credentials, exposing source code, API keys, and employee data. Iranian APT group Seedworm is targeting global organizations via Microsoft Teams phishing, while British hacker Tyler Buchanan (linked to Scattered Spider) pled guilty to an $8M hacking scheme. Canada Life Assurance Company appears on a ShinyHunters victim list.

canada_landscape.summary: Canadian organizations are under active threat. The Canada Life Assurance Company appears on a ShinyHunters victim list following the group's breach spree. No specific CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period, though German and Canadian authorities (BSI and BfV) are warning of state-sponsored phishing attacks via Microsoft Teams. Canadian retail, financial, and infrastructure sectors should heighten vigilance given the Vercel breach and ongoing Microsoft Teams phishing campaigns.
canada_landscape.retailers: No major Canadian retail chains (Loblaws, Canadian Tire, SportChek, Shoppers Drug Mart) directly identified in breaches this period. Basic-Fit (200,000 Dutch members) was breached by ShinyHunters, indicating retail sector targeting.
canada_landscape.financial: The Canada Life Assurance Company (canadalife.com) confirmed victim of ShinyHunters breach. No incidents reported for CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, or BMO in this reporting period.
